title: Evangelion-VOX
wikitext:
{{Media Infobox
| name      =  Evangelion Vox
| image     = [[Image:Vox.jpg|200px]]
| publisher = {{Publisher|JP=King Records/Starchild}}
| release   = {{Release|JP=December 3, 1997}}
| media     = Audio CD
| author    = [[Shiro Sagisu]]
}}
''Evangelion-VOX'' is an album consisting of songs from and based on the Neon Genesis Evangelion TV series and the movie ''[[The End of Evangelion]]''. It was recorded in 1997 and released December 3, 1997. The songs are mostly R&B/hip-hop remakes of several pieces of background music from the ''Evangelion'' series, with additional rap tracks written and performed by [[Mali]], and bookended by spoken word tracks. The album was released by Starchild Records and produced by [[Shirō Sagisu]] with assistance from Martin Lascelles (the two components of the "Mash" in [[Loren and Mash]]). It peaked at number 10 on the Oricon albums chart, making 6 appearances.

Author Shiro Sagisu

Evangelion-VOX is an album consisting of songs from and based on the Neon Genesis Evangelion TV series and the movie The End of Evangelion. It was recorded in 1997 and released December 3, 1997. The songs are mostly R&B/hip-hop remakes of several pieces of background music from the Evangelion series, with additional rap tracks written and performed by Mali, and bookended by spoken word tracks. The album was released by Starchild Records and produced by Shirō Sagisu with assistance from Martin Lascelles (the two components of the "Mash" in Loren and Mash). It peaked at number 10 on the Oricon albums chart, making 6 appearances.
